highlight.title: Major UK Education White Paper 2026: New National Curriculum Coming 2028
highlight.summary: <cite index="2-2,4-11">The government has confirmed a revised curriculum will be published in 2027, for first teaching in 2028, following completion of the national curriculum and assessment review.</cite> <cite index="5-2,5-3">The refreshed National Curriculum will emphasise literacy, numeracy, oracy, and digital/media literacy, with Progress 8 being reshaped to reflect this wider focus.</cite>

resources.title: KS2 SATs 2026: Complete Date Guide & Revision Priority Topics
resources.why_now: For parents with Year 6 children - <cite index="21-15,21-16">SATs run from Monday 11th May to Thursday 14th May 2026</cite> and <cite index="23-1,23-3">revision should focus on Calculations and Fractions, Decimals and Percentages as they make up the biggest proportions historically</cite>.

resources.title: Oak National Academy Free Lessons and Resources
resources.why_now: For students across all year groups - <cite index="12-1,12-2">browse and find free videos, quizzes and lessons from KS1 to KS4, just find your year group, subject and lesson</cite> with comprehensive curriculum coverage during ongoing education reforms.

resources.title: Active GCSE & A-Level Revision Techniques That Actually Work
resources.why_now: For GCSE and A-Level students - <cite index="31-12">completing past papers under timed exam conditions, practising exam-style questions regularly, using flashcards to test key terms, and explaining concepts aloud</cite> are proven active techniques that strengthen memory recall.

resources.title: Computing Curriculum Changes Coming 2026: What Schools Need to Know
resources.why_now: For parents of secondary students - <cite index="8-20,8-21">artificial intelligence, machine learning, cybersecurity threats have moved on dramatically since 2014, with the curriculum review aiming to update what children learn about technology they will actually use as adults</cite>.

claude_take: The UK education system is experiencing its biggest shake-up in decades with the 2026 White Paper promising fundamental curriculum changes by 2028. For families, this means focusing on proven revision strategies now while staying informed about upcoming changes that will reshape how children learn from primary through to A-Levels.
